notes: Only counts as active when explicit numbered DOE rows or governed deterministic recovery rows actually enter the run-local Stage2 candidate surface.
Guard belongs to the active Stage2 run contract and should be emitted by the same run that writes the weak-label TSV.

Activation is evidence-anchor based; when table_row anchors are not exposed reliably, the feature should stay unclear rather than assumed active. Governed recovery rows count when they preserve the same explicit table-row anchor pattern.
Stage2 input assembly may switch to an ordered evidence pack when the governed execution mode is enabled; activation is only credited when the controlled order is proven by run artifacts.
Maintained Stage2 runs must persist candidate_blocks_v1.json with section-aware prose segmentation before the role-aware selector consumes the candidate surface.
Expected when table assets exist for the paper; activation is credited from candidate-level artifacts rather than from downstream selector debug alone.
Candidate-level noise filtering is governed only when run-local candidate artifacts or debug surfaces show the maintained pre-selector filtering path.
The governed S2-2 boundary must persist evidence_blocks_v1.json as the canonical pre-LLM evidence artifact for maintained Stage2 runs.
The canonical S2-2 artifact must distinguish technical completeness from design-conformance status before downstream prompt assembly can be treated as normal.
The maintained Stage2 prompt preview is a derived observability surface only and must resolve back to evidence_blocks_v1.json rather than stand alone.
Maintained Stage2 runs must use the deterministic role-aware S2-2 selector and persist role assignments plus missing_or_weak_roles in the canonical evidence artifact.
Expected only when the maintained S2-2 pre-LLM signals indicate a DOE or optimization paper; activation must be evidenced in the canonical artifact rather than assumed from code.
Activation should be credited only when a run-local evidence artifact records an actual duplicate-table suppression event, not merely because the selector supports it.
Diagnostic parse-repair unit; evidence is the frozen raw parse audit and comparison artifacts, not downstream stage outputs.
Diagnostic fallback basis; evidence is the Path 1 failure subset and Path 2 parse audit, not a mainline pipeline artifact.
Feature exists only when benchmark comparison artifacts prove the variant-aware GT workbook was actually used.
Parent runs should not inherit activation credit from Stage5 child replays; activation must come from the run-local final-output artifacts.
The registry plus intervention matrix should be reflected in run-local activation reporting, but code presence alone does not prove run activation.
RUN_CONTEXT.md should expose the activation report path, required features, and run gate for benchmark-relevant runs.
The compare node should emit a DOI-level count table whenever a benchmark compare is present.
